skill.name: smc-chart-analysis
skill.description: Interprets Smart Money Concepts and ICT-style chart data from a local SMC analysis API. It explains market structure, liquidity, fair value gaps, order blocks, and possible trade setups with risk notes.
skill.summary: Interpret SMC and ICT chart analysis with structured trade setup narratives.
skill.author: AaronAbuUsama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found most hits are Markdown examples or SMC trading terms such as BSL and SSL, not cryptography or system probing. The real publish-time concern is that the skill instructs assistants to run curl and bun commands for a localhost API, so user-supplied symbols should be encoded and commands should be reviewed before execution.

medium_findings.title: Shell Commands for Local API Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run curl commands against localhost and a bun dev command for a local project. This appears aligned with the chart-analysis workflow, but it is still external command execution and can become risky if user-supplied symbols are interpolated without URL encoding or shell review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and intentional, and they use fixed localhost endpoints in the examples. Confidence is below very high because no executable source code shows actual interpolation behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown and Trading Terminology Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts are caused by Markdown code fences and inline backticks, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match SMC terms such as BSL, SSL, sweeps, and price levels rather than security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples and trading glossary entries, not executable code. No evidence found of cryptographic operations or host reconnaissance in these contexts.

low_findings.title: Educational External Links
low_findings.description: The references file contains hardcoded links to YouTube, TradingView, GitHub, and education sites. These are user-facing learning resources and do not show automatic network requests or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a learning-resource document and are not invoked by code. This supports a low-severity classification rather than a confirmed network risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands for Local API Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run curl commands against localhost and a bun dev command for a local project. This appears aligned with the chart-analysis workflow, but it is still external command execution and can become risky if user-supplied symbols are interpolated without URL encoding or shell review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and intentional, and they use fixed localhost endpoints in the examples. Confidence is below very high because no executable source code shows actual interpolation behavior.

content.value_statement: SMC and ICT chart analysis can be difficult to turn into a clear trade plan. This skill converts local detector output into bias, liquidity, entry, stop, target, and risk commentary.
actual_capabilities: Fetches SMC detector output from a localhost analysis API when the local app is available.
Interprets market structure using bias, premium or discount zones, and liquidity levels.
Identifies possible order blocks, fair value gaps, sweeps, and change of character signals.
Builds trade setup narratives with direction, entry zone, stop loss, targets, and risk ratios.
Grades setups with confluence checks and plain-language warnings.
limitations: Requires a local SMC web app or prepared detector output to provide current market data.
Does not guarantee profitable trades or replace independent risk management.
Supports only the symbols, timeframes, and data sources exposed by the local analysis API.
SMC and ICT concepts are interpretive and may conflict with other trading methods.

use_cases.title: Crypto Trader Review
use_cases.description: Review BTC, ETH, or SOL structure before planning entries, stops, and liquidity targets.

use_cases.title: Trading Coach Explanation
use_cases.description: Convert detector output into a clear explanation of sweeps, FVGs, order blocks, and bias.

use_cases.title: Developer Output Testing
use_cases.description: Check whether SMC detector responses produce useful human-readable setup narratives.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Symbol
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single market view
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze BTC with the SMC chart analysis skill. Include bias, key liquidity, setup grade, warnings, and risk notes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Timeframes
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze ETH using 4H for bias and 15M for entry timing. Explain any conflict between higher and lower timeframes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Trade Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the latest SMC detector data for SOL and build a long and short scenario with entry zones, stops, targets, invalidation, and confluence.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Detector Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of supplied analysis data
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this SMC detector output, identify the strongest setup, explain why weaker setups should be avoided, and state what confirmation is still missing.

output_examples.output: BTC shows a bullish structure shift, but price is in premium. The skill would suggest waiting for a pullback into a fair value gap before considering a long setup, with stops below recent structure and targets at buy-side liquidity.
The response would summarize unswept buy-side and sell-side levels, recent sweeps, and the nearest liquidity target. It would avoid a prediction and present bullish and bearish confirmation levels.

output_examples.input: Create a setup plan from supplied detector output.
output_examples.output: The skill would provide a setup grade, direction, entry zone, stop loss, multiple targets, confluence checklist, warnings, and a short narrative explaining the trade thesis.

best_practices: Use fresh detector output and confirm the symbol, timeframe, and market session before analysis.
Ask for both bullish and bearish scenarios when bias is unclear or price is near equilibrium.
Treat every setup as analysis only and define invalidation before considering execution.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to guarantee a price move or promise profit.
Do not run shell commands with untrusted symbols unless the URL parameters are encoded and reviewed.
Do not ignore warnings about premium, discount, missing confluence, or low-quality setup grades.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and trade planning language only. It does not execute orders.

faq.question: Does it need live market data?
faq.answer: It needs detector output from the local SMC API or data supplied in the prompt.

faq.question: Which markets can it analyze?
faq.answer: It can analyze markets supported by the local API, with examples for BTC, ETH, and SOL.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. The output is technical analysis and should be reviewed with independent risk controls.

faq.question: Why does it mention liquidity sweeps?
faq.answer: SMC analysis focuses on stop clusters near highs and lows, often called buy-side and sell-side liquidity.

faq.question: Can I use it without the local web app?
faq.answer: Yes, if you paste equivalent SMC detector output into the prompt for the assistant to interpret.
